name: Signet Notification Channel
description: Webhook-based authorization channel for Tier 3 requests and ANOMALY escalations. Presents structured reasoning payload: who is asking, what they want, why it is unusual, what options the user has. Accepts user response: approve, deny, or modify (with scope adjustment). Timeout defaults to deny (fail-secure). Sub-second delivery target for webhooks. Implements webhook delivery with retry logic, HMAC-SHA256 signature verification for callback authenticity. Response propagation back to the suspended policy decision. SMS and push channels are out of scope for v1.

types.name: ChallengeId

fields.name: value
fields.description: URL-safe base64-encoded 128-bit random identifier

validators.error_message: ChallengeId must be between 16 and 64 characters
ChallengeId must be URL-safe base64

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a unique challenge identifier string. Not interchangeable with DeliveryId or EventId. Generated as a cryptographically random 128-bit value encoded as URL-safe base64. Implements Display, Clone, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize.

types.name: DeliveryId

fields.name: value
fields.description: URL-safe base64-encoded 128-bit random identifier

validators.error_message: DeliveryId must be between 16 and 64 characters
DeliveryId must be URL-safe base64

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a unique webhook delivery attempt identifier. Not interchangeable with ChallengeId or EventId. Generated per delivery attempt. Implements Display, Clone, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize.

types.name: EventId

fields.name: value
fields.description: URL-safe base64-encoded 128-bit random identifier

validators.error_message: EventId must be between 16 and 64 characters
EventId must be URL-safe base64

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a unique notification event identifier. Correlates the logical authorization event across retries and audit records. Not interchangeable with ChallengeId or DeliveryId. Implements Display, Clone, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize.

types.name: Timestamp

fields.name: rfc3339
fields.description: RFC 3339 formatted UTC timestamp string

validators.error_message: Timestamp must be RFC 3339 UTC format

types.description: RFC 3339 formatted timestamp. Serialized as a string in JSON. Uses the `time` crate OffsetDateTime internally.

types.name: Duration

fields.name: seconds
fields.description: Duration in seconds, must be non-negative

validators.error_message: Duration must be non-negative

types.description: A non-negative duration in whole seconds, used for timeouts and TTLs.

types.name: ScopeEntry

fields.name: resource
fields.description: Hierarchical resource path (e.g., 'vault.financial.tax_returns')

validators.error_message: Resource path must be between 1 and 512 characters

fields.name: action
fields.description: Action on the resource (e.g., 'read', 'prove', 'disclose')

validators.error_message: Action must be between 1 and 128 characters

types.description: A single scope permission entry, representing a resource path and an action. Used within ScopeSet for subset validation on Modify responses.

types.name: ScopeSet

fields.name: entries
fields.description: The scope entries in this set, deduplicated by (resource, action) pair

validators.error_message: ScopeSet must contain at least one entry

types.description: An ordered, deduplicated set of ScopeEntry values. First-class type that enforces subset validation: a modified scope must be a strict subset of the originally requested scope. Prevents privilege escalation on Modify responses. Implements PartialOrd for subset checks.

types.name: ScopeEntryList
types.description: A list of ScopeEntry values.

types.name: RequesterInfo

fields.name: service_name
fields.description: Human-readable name of the requesting service

validators.error_message: Service name must be between 1 and 256 characters

fields.name: service_identifier
fields.description: Unique machine identifier for the requesting service (e.g., DID, domain)

fields.name: agent_session_id
fields.description: Identifier of the agent session mediating this request

fields.name: request_timestamp
fields.description: When the original request was received by the agent

types.description: Structured information about who is making the request. Presented to the user as part of the authorization reasoning payload.

types.name: AnomalyContext

fields.name: anomaly_type
fields.description: Category of the anomaly (e.g., 'role_mismatch', 'unusual_time', 'excessive_scope', 'frequency_spike')

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity classification of the anomaly

fields.name: explanation
fields.description: Human-readable explanation of why this is anomalous

validators.error_message: Explanation must be between 1 and 2048 characters

fields.name: evidence_summary
fields.description: Summary of the evidence that triggered the anomaly detection, with no raw credential data

fields.name: policy_rule_id
fields.description: Identifier of the policy rule that triggered the escalation

types.description: Structured context explaining why a request was flagged as anomalous. Presented to the user for informed decision-making.

types.name: AnomalySeverity
types.description: Severity levels for anomaly escalations. Determines default timeout and UI urgency hints.

types.name: AvailableActions

fields.name: can_approve
fields.description: Whether the user may approve the request as-is

fields.name: can_deny
fields.description: Whether the user may deny the request (always true)

fields.name: can_modify
fields.description: Whether the user may modify (reduce) the scope

fields.name: suggested_scope
fields.description: If can_modify is true, a suggested reduced scope the user might accept. Absent if modification is not available.

types.description: Describes the set of actions available to the user for a given authorization request. Always includes approve and deny; may include modify with a suggested reduced scope.

types.name: OptionalScopeSet
types.description: An optional ScopeSet, present when scope modification is available.

types.name: Tier3AccessPayload

fields.name: requester
fields.description: Who is asking for access

fields.name: compartment_label
fields.description: Human-readable label for the Tier 3 compartment (e.g., 'Medical Records', 'Financial Documents')

fields.name: requested_scope
fields.description: Exact scope of data access being requested

fields.name: justification
fields.description: Machine-generated or service-provided justification for the request

validators.error_message: Justification must be between 1 and 4096 characters

fields.name: available_actions
fields.description: Actions the user can take on this request

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: When this authorization request expires (deny on timeout)

types.description: Payload for a Tier 3 compartment access authorization request. Contains all structured reasoning for the user.

types.name: AnomalyEscalationPayload

fields.name: requester
fields.description: Who is asking

fields.name: original_tier
fields.description: The tier of the original request that was flagged (1 or 2, since Tier 3 always requires explicit auth)

validators.error_message: Original tier for anomaly escalation must be 1 or 2

fields.name: requested_scope
fields.description: Scope of the flagged request

fields.name: anomaly_context
fields.description: Structured explanation of the anomaly

fields.name: available_actions
fields.description: Actions the user can take

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: When this escalation expires (deny on timeout)

types.description: Payload for an anomaly escalation authorization request. Contains structured reasoning about why the request is unusual.

types.name: AuthorizationRequest

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Unique event identifier correlating this authorization across retries and audit

fields.name: challenge_id
fields.description: Challenge identifier for callback correlation

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Variant-specific payload: Tier3AccessPayload or AnomalyEscalationPayload

types.description: Internally-tagged serde enum representing an authorization request. Serialized with serde tag='request_type' and snake_case rename. Two variants: Tier3Access and AnomalyEscalation.

types.name: AuthorizationRequestPayload
types.description: Union of authorization request payload variants, discriminated by the AuthorizationRequest tag.

types.name: AuthorizationResponse

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Human-readable reason for denial. Present only for Deny variant. Optional even for Deny.

fields.name: adjusted_scope
fields.description: Adjusted (reduced) scope. Present only for Modify variant. Must be a strict subset of the original requested_scope.

types.description: Wire-format response from the user via webhook callback. Deserialized with deny_unknown_fields. Three variants: Approve (no additional data), Deny (with optional reason string), Modify (with adjusted_scope that must be a subset of the original requested_scope).

types.name: DenialReason
types.description: Internal reason for a denial decision. Distinguishes timeout-based denials (fail-secure), explicit user denials, and delivery failures.

types.name: AuthorizationDecision

fields.name: challenge_id
fields.description: The challenge this decision resolves

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Correlation event identifier

fields.name: decided_at
fields.description: When the decision was finalized

fields.name: denial_reason
fields.description: Present for Denied variant, absent for Approved/Modified

fields.name: denial_message
fields.description: Human-readable denial reason from user, if provided. Empty for Approved/Modified and auto-denials.

fields.name: adjusted_scope
fields.description: Present for Modified variant, absent for Approved/Denied

types.description: Internal validated decision type returned by ChallengeHandle::resolve(). Distinct from the wire-format AuthorizationResponse. Carries full provenance for audit.

types.name: OptionalDenialReason
types.description: An optional DenialReason.

types.name: ChallengeHandle

fields.name: challenge_id
fields.description: The challenge identifier this handle tracks

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Correlation event identifier

fields.name: deadline
fields.description: Absolute deadline after which this challenge resolves as Denied

fields.name: original_scope
fields.description: The original requested scope, retained for subset validation of Modify responses

types.description: Fail-secure handle to a pending authorization challenge. Wraps a tokio oneshot receiver with a deadline. Consumed (moved) on resolution to prevent double-resolution. If the deadline expires before a response is received, resolves as Denied with DenialReason::Timeout. Once resolved, the handle is gone — it cannot be polled again.

types.name: WebhookSignatureHeaders

fields.name: webhook_id
fields.description: Value of Signet-Webhook-Id header. Unique per delivery attempt.

fields.name: webhook_timestamp
fields.description: Value of Signet-Webhook-Timestamp header. Time of this delivery attempt.

fields.name: webhook_signature
fields.description: Value of Signet-Webhook-Signature header. HMAC-SHA256 over (webhook_id || webhook_timestamp || body), base64-encoded.

validators.error_message: Webhook signature must be versioned base64 (v1,<base64>)

types.description: The three Signet-namespaced headers included with every webhook delivery for HMAC signature verification.

types.name: WebhookSecret

fields.name: key_bytes
fields.description: Raw HMAC key material. Zeroized on drop.

validators.error_message: Webhook secret must be at least 256 bits (32 bytes)

fields.name: key_id
fields.description: Identifier for this key, used to select the correct key during rotation

types.description: HMAC signing secret for webhook signatures. Implements Zeroize and ZeroizeOnDrop — secret material is zeroed from memory when this value is dropped. 256-bit minimum entropy.

types.name: EndpointConfig

fields.name: url
fields.description: HTTPS URL of the webhook endpoint. Must be HTTPS.

validators.error_message: Webhook endpoint must use HTTPS
Webhook URL must be between 9 and 2048 characters

fields.name: current_secret
fields.description: Current HMAC signing secret

fields.name: previous_secret
fields.description: Previous HMAC signing secret, valid during rotation window. Absent if not rotating.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Per-request HTTP timeout for webhook delivery

fields.name: max_retries
fields.description: Maximum number of retry attempts before giving up

validators.error_message: Max retries must be between 0 and 10

fields.name: circuit_breaker_threshold
fields.description: Number of consecutive failures before the circuit breaker opens

validators.error_message: Circuit breaker threshold must be between 1 and 100

types.description: Configuration for a webhook delivery endpoint. Supports dual-secret rotation: both current_secret and previous_secret are valid for signature verification during rotation windows. Circuit breaker tracks health.

types.name: OptionalWebhookSecret
types.description: An optional WebhookSecret, used for the previous secret during rotation.

types.name: EndpointHealth

fields.name: consecutive_failures
fields.description: Number of consecutive delivery failures

fields.name: circuit_state
fields.description: Current circuit breaker state

fields.name: last_success
fields.description: Timestamp of the last successful delivery, if any

fields.name: last_failure
fields.description: Timestamp of the last failed delivery, if any

fields.name: total_deliveries
fields.description: Total number of delivery attempts

fields.name: total_failures
fields.description: Total number of failed delivery attempts

types.description: Health status of a webhook endpoint, tracked by the circuit breaker.

types.name: OptionalTimestamp
types.description: An optional Timestamp.

types.name: CircuitState
types.description: Circuit breaker states for webhook endpoint health.

types.name: RetryPolicy

fields.name: initial_delay
fields.description: Initial delay before the first retry

fields.name: max_delay
fields.description: Maximum delay between retries (cap on exponential growth)

fields.name: backoff_factor
fields.description: Multiplicative factor for exponential backoff (e.g., 2.0)

validators.error_message: Backoff factor must be between 1.0 and 10.0

fields.name: jitter_fraction
fields.description: Fraction of the delay to randomize as jitter (0.0 to 1.0)

validators.error_message: Jitter fraction must be between 0.0 and 1.0

types.description: Exponential backoff retry configuration with jitter. Retries are bounded by both max_retries and the challenge deadline (whichever comes first).

types.name: DeliveryAttempt

fields.name: delivery_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this delivery attempt

fields.name: challenge_id
fields.description: Challenge this delivery is for

fields.name: attempt_number
fields.description: 1-indexed attempt number

validators.error_message: Attempt number must be at least 1

fields.name: attempted_at
fields.description: When this attempt was made

fields.name: outcome
fields.description: Outcome of this delivery attempt

fields.name: http_status
fields.description: HTTP status code returned, if the request completed

fields.name: latency_ms
fields.description: Round-trip latency in milliseconds

types.description: Record of a single webhook delivery attempt, used for audit and debugging.

types.name: OptionalInt
types.description: An optional integer.

types.name: DeliveryOutcome
types.description: Outcome of a single webhook delivery attempt.

types.name: DeliveryReport

fields.name: challenge_id
fields.description: Challenge this report covers

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Correlation event identifier

fields.name: final_outcome
fields.description: Final outcome after all retries

fields.name: attempts
fields.description: All delivery attempts in order

fields.name: total_duration_ms
fields.description: Total wall-clock time across all attempts in milliseconds

types.description: Full delivery report for a challenge, aggregating all attempts.

types.name: DeliveryAttemptList
types.description: A list of DeliveryAttempt records.

types.name: CallbackPayload

fields.name: challenge_token
fields.description: PASETO v4.local token encoding the challenge_id and expiration. Verified via rusty_paseto.

validators.error_message: Challenge token must be a PASETO v4.local token

fields.name: response
fields.description: The user's authorization decision

types.description: Payload received on the callback endpoint from the user's webhook consumer. Contains the PASETO challenge token for verification and the user's authorization response.

types.name: NotifyError
types.description: Error type for the signet-notify crate. Derived via thiserror. All messages are generic to prevent secret leakage and timing oracles. Crypto failures produce the same generic error regardless of cause.

types.name: NotifyResult

fields.name: value
fields.description: Success value of generic type T

fields.name: error
fields.description: Error, if the operation failed

types.description: Type alias representation: Result<T, NotifyError>. All public functions return this type.

types.name: OptionalNotifyError
types.description: An optional NotifyError.

types.name: ChallengeRegistrySnapshot

fields.name: active_challenges
fields.description: Number of currently pending challenges

fields.name: expired_challenges_cleaned
fields.description: Number of expired challenges cleaned up since last snapshot

fields.name: total_challenges_issued
fields.description: Total challenges issued since registry creation

fields.name: total_challenges_resolved
fields.description: Total challenges resolved (by response, timeout, or cleanup)

types.description: Diagnostic snapshot of the ChallengeRegistry state. No secret material is included.

functions.name: notify
functions.description: Primary integration point for the policy engine. Sends an authorization request through the notification channel and returns a ChallengeHandle that will resolve to an AuthorizationDecision. This is the sole method on the NotificationChannel trait. The returned ChallengeHandle is fail-secure: if no response is received by the deadline, it resolves as Denied with DenialReason::Timeout. The method is async and initiates webhook delivery but does not wait for the user's response.

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: The authorization request to send to the user

error_cases.name: endpoint_unavailable
error_cases.condition: No valid endpoint configuration is available

error_cases.name: circuit_breaker_open
error_cases.condition: The endpoint circuit breaker is in Open state and no half-open probe is due

error_cases.name: expired_request
error_cases.condition: request.payload.expires_at is in the past or too close to now to be useful

error_cases.name: configuration_error
error_cases.condition: Endpoint configuration is invalid (e.g., non-HTTPS URL, missing secret)

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected internal failure (e.g., channel creation failed)

preconditions: Endpoint configuration is present and valid
Circuit breaker is not in Open state (or half-open probe is due)
postconditions: A ChallengeHandle is returned that will resolve to exactly one AuthorizationDecision
The challenge is registered in the ChallengeRegistry
Webhook delivery has been initiated (but not necessarily completed)
An EventId-correlated audit record has been created
The ChallengeHandle deadline matches request.payload.expires_at
If the ChallengeHandle is dropped without calling resolve(), the challenge is cleaned up
side_effects: Initiates async HTTP POST to the configured webhook endpoint
Creates audit record for the notification event

structured_side_effects.description: HTTP POST with JSON payload and HMAC signature headers
Inserts challenge_id -> oneshot::Sender mapping
Records notification event with event_id, challenge_id, request type, timestamp. No payload content logged.

functions.name: resolve
functions.description: Consumes the ChallengeHandle and returns the final AuthorizationDecision. Races the internal oneshot receiver against the deadline. If the deadline expires first, returns Denied with DenialReason::Timeout. If the sender is dropped (delivery failure), returns Denied with DenialReason::DeliveryFailure. The handle is moved (consumed) — this method can only be called once. After resolution, the challenge is removed from the registry.

inputs.name: handle
inputs.description: The challenge handle to resolve. Consumed by this call.

preconditions: handle has not been previously resolved (enforced by move semantics)
handle is a valid ChallengeHandle obtained from notify()
postconditions: Exactly one AuthorizationDecision is returned
The ChallengeHandle is consumed and cannot be used again
The challenge is removed from the ChallengeRegistry
If deadline expired, decision is Denied with DenialReason::Timeout
If sender was dropped, decision is Denied with DenialReason::DeliveryFailure
If response was Modify, the adjusted_scope has been validated as a subset of original_scope
decided_at timestamp reflects the actual resolution time
ANOMALY escalations that timeout produce a Denied decision (never silently resolved)

structured_side_effects.description: Removes the challenge_id entry on resolution
Records authorization decision with event_id, challenge_id, decision variant, decided_at. No scope content logged.

functions.name: handle_callback
functions.description: Processes an incoming webhook callback from the user. Verifies the PASETO v4 challenge token, validates the response (including subset check for Modify), and completes the oneshot channel to unblock the corresponding ChallengeHandle::resolve(). This is the entry point for the callback HTTP handler.

inputs.name: challenge_id
inputs.description: The challenge being responded to, extracted from the callback URL or payload

inputs.name: callback
inputs.description: The callback payload containing the PASETO token and user response

error_cases.name: challenge_not_found
error_cases.condition: No pending challenge with the given challenge_id exists in the registry

error_cases.name: challenge_expired
error_cases.condition: The challenge deadline has passed

error_cases.name: challenge_already_resolved
error_cases.condition: The challenge has already received a response

error_cases.name: invalid_token
error_cases.condition: The PASETO v4 challenge token is invalid, expired, or does not match the challenge_id

error_cases.name: invalid_response
error_cases.condition: The AuthorizationResponse fails deserialization or validation (e.g., unknown fields, invalid variant)

error_cases.name: scope_escalation
error_cases.condition: A Modify response contains an adjusted_scope that is not a strict subset of the original requested_scope

preconditions: challenge_id corresponds to a currently pending challenge in the registry
callback.challenge_token is a valid PASETO v4.local token
The challenge has not already been resolved
postconditions: The oneshot sender for the challenge has been completed with the validated response
The corresponding ChallengeHandle::resolve() will now return with the user's decision
If response is Modify, adjusted_scope is a strict subset of original requested_scope
The callback PASETO token has been consumed (replay protection)
side_effects: Completes the oneshot channel sender for the challenge
Records callback receipt in audit log

structured_side_effects.description: Sends the validated AuthorizationResponse through the oneshot channel
Records callback receipt with challenge_id, response variant, timestamp. No scope or token content logged.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of scope entries for subset validation

functions.name: verify_webhook_signature
functions.description: Verifies the HMAC-SHA256 signature of an incoming webhook callback request. Uses constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks. Supports dual-secret verification during key rotation: tries current_secret first, falls back to previous_secret if present.

inputs.name: headers
inputs.description: The three Signet-namespaced headers from the HTTP request

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Raw HTTP request body bytes

inputs.name: endpoint_config
inputs.description: Endpoint configuration containing the signing secret(s)

error_cases.name: invalid_signature_format
error_cases.condition: The webhook_signature header is malformed or uses an unsupported version prefix

error_cases.name: configuration_error
error_cases.condition: The endpoint_config has no valid secrets configured

preconditions: headers.webhook_timestamp is within acceptable clock skew tolerance (±5 minutes)
postconditions: Returns true if and only if the signature matches the body signed with either current_secret or previous_secret
Comparison is constant-time regardless of which byte differs
Stale timestamps (beyond tolerance) always return false even if signature is valid

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation, no side effects

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the body length

functions.name: sign_webhook_payload
functions.description: Generates HMAC-SHA256 signature headers for an outgoing webhook delivery. Creates the DeliveryId, timestamps the delivery, computes the HMAC over (delivery_id || timestamp || body), and returns the complete set of signature headers.

inputs.name: body
inputs.description: Serialized JSON request body to sign

inputs.name: secret
inputs.description: The HMAC signing secret to use

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: HMAC computation fails (e.g., aws-lc-rs error)

postconditions: Returned headers contain a fresh DeliveryId
webhook_timestamp reflects the current time
webhook_signature is HMAC-SHA256(secret, delivery_id || timestamp || body) base64-encoded with v1 prefix
verify_webhook_signature(returned_headers, body, config_with_same_secret) returns true

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation except for DeliveryId generation which reads from CSPRNG

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the body length

functions.name: deliver_webhook
functions.description: Delivers a signed webhook payload to the configured endpoint with retry logic. Uses exponential backoff with jitter, bounded by both max_retries and the challenge deadline. Updates endpoint health (circuit breaker) based on outcomes. Returns a full DeliveryReport. Sub-second delivery target for the first attempt.

inputs.name: challenge_id
inputs.description: Challenge this delivery is for

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.description: Correlation event identifier

inputs.name: payload_json
inputs.description: Serialized JSON body of the AuthorizationRequest

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.description: Target endpoint configuration

inputs.name: retry_policy
inputs.description: Retry configuration for this delivery

inputs.name: deadline
inputs.description: Absolute deadline for all delivery attempts (matches challenge deadline)

error_cases.name: endpoint_unavailable
error_cases.condition: DNS resolution or TCP connection fails on all attempts

error_cases.name: circuit_breaker_open
error_cases.condition: Circuit breaker is in Open state before first attempt

error_cases.name: delivery_failed
error_cases.condition: All retry attempts exhausted or deadline reached without successful delivery

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected failure during signing or HTTP client operation

preconditions: endpoint.url is a valid HTTPS URL
Circuit breaker is not in Open state (checked before first attempt)
postconditions: DeliveryReport contains at least one DeliveryAttempt
All delivery attempts occurred before the deadline
final_outcome reflects the outcome of the last attempt
Endpoint health (circuit breaker) has been updated based on outcomes
If all attempts fail and deadline has not passed, retries followed exponential backoff with jitter
Each attempt used a fresh DeliveryId and HMAC signature
side_effects: Makes HTTP POST requests to the webhook endpoint
Updates endpoint health / circuit breaker state
Records delivery attempts in audit log

structured_side_effects.description: HTTP POST with JSON body and Signet-Webhook-* signature headers per attempt
Updates consecutive_failures, circuit_state, last_success/last_failure, totals
Records each delivery attempt with delivery_id, attempt_number, outcome, latency. No payload content logged.

functions.name: create_challenge_token
functions.description: Creates a PASETO v4.local token encoding the challenge_id and expiration for callback verification. The token is included in the webhook payload so the user's webhook consumer can present it on callback, proving the callback is authentic and tied to a specific challenge.

inputs.name: challenge_id
inputs.description: The challenge to encode in the token

inputs.name: expires_at
inputs.description: Token expiration, matching the challenge deadline

inputs.name: symmetric_key
inputs.description: PASETO v4.local symmetric encryption key (256-bit). Zeroized after use.

validators.error_message: PASETO v4.local key must be exactly 32 bytes (256 bits)

error_cases.name: internal_error
error_cases.condition: PASETO token creation fails (e.g., rusty_paseto error)

postconditions: Returned string is a valid PASETO v4.local token
Token claims include challenge_id and exp (expiration)
Token can be verified by verify_challenge_token with the same symmetric_key
symmetric_key input is zeroized from caller's memory after use

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation

functions.name: verify_challenge_token
functions.description: Verifies a PASETO v4.local challenge token from a webhook callback. Decrypts the token, validates expiration, and extracts the challenge_id. Returns the challenge_id if valid. Uses constant-time operations internally (provided by rusty_paseto).

inputs.name: token
inputs.description: The PASETO v4.local token string to verify

validators.error_message: Token must be a PASETO v4.local token

inputs.name: expected_challenge_id
inputs.description: The challenge_id we expect to find in the token (must match)

inputs.name: symmetric_key
inputs.description: PASETO v4.local symmetric decryption key (256-bit). Zeroized after use.

validators.error_message: PASETO v4.local key must be exactly 32 bytes (256 bits)

error_cases.name: invalid_token
error_cases.condition: Token decryption fails, is malformed, or claims are invalid

error_cases.name: challenge_id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Token's challenge_id does not match expected_challenge_id

error_cases.name: token_expired
error_cases.condition: Token's exp claim is in the past

preconditions: token is a well-formed PASETO v4.local string
postconditions: Returned ChallengeId matches expected_challenge_id and the token's embedded challenge_id
Token has not expired (exp claim is in the future)
Token was encrypted with the provided symmetric_key
symmetric_key input is zeroized from caller's memory after use

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation

functions.name: validate_scope_subset
functions.description: Validates that a proposed adjusted scope is a strict subset of the original requested scope. Used to prevent privilege escalation on Modify responses. A strict subset means every entry in adjusted_scope appears in original_scope, and adjusted_scope has fewer entries than original_scope.

inputs.name: original_scope
inputs.description: The originally requested scope

inputs.name: adjusted_scope
inputs.description: The proposed adjusted (reduced) scope from a Modify response

postconditions: Returns true if and only if adjusted_scope is a strict subset of original_scope
Strict subset means: every (resource, action) pair in adjusted_scope exists in original_scope, AND adjusted_scope has strictly fewer entries
An equal set returns false (not strictly less)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n * m) where n is adjusted_scope entries and m is original_scope entries; O(n + m) with hash set optimization

functions.name: register_challenge
functions.description: Registers a new challenge in the ChallengeRegistry. Creates a oneshot channel pair, stores the sender keyed by challenge_id, and returns the ChallengeHandle wrapping the receiver. Called internally by notify().

inputs.name: challenge_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier for this challenge

inputs.name: event_id
inputs.description: Correlation event identifier

inputs.name: deadline
inputs.description: Absolute deadline for this challenge

inputs.name: original_scope
inputs.description: The original requested scope, retained for Modify subset validation

error_cases.name: duplicate_challenge
error_cases.condition: A challenge with this challenge_id is already registered

preconditions: challenge_id is not already registered in the ChallengeRegistry
postconditions: ChallengeRegistry contains an entry mapping challenge_id to the oneshot::Sender
Returned ChallengeHandle wraps the corresponding oneshot::Receiver
ChallengeHandle.deadline matches the provided deadline
ChallengeHandle.original_scope matches the provided original_scope
total_challenges_issued is incremented by 1

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts challenge_id -> oneshot::Sender<AuthorizationResponse> mapping

functions.name: cleanup_expired_challenges
functions.description: Scans the ChallengeRegistry for expired challenges and removes them. Drops the oneshot::Sender for each expired challenge, which causes the corresponding ChallengeHandle::resolve() to return Denied with DenialReason::DeliveryFailure if it hasn't already resolved. Should be called periodically (e.g., on a timer).

inputs.name: now
inputs.description: Current time to compare against challenge deadlines

postconditions: All challenges with deadline < now have been removed from the registry
Their oneshot::Senders have been dropped
Returned int is the count of challenges cleaned up
expired_challenges_cleaned counter is incremented by the returned count

structured_side_effects.description: Removes all expired challenge entries and drops their senders
Logs count of expired challenges cleaned up, with their challenge_ids

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of registered challenges

functions.name: get_registry_snapshot
functions.description: Returns a diagnostic snapshot of the ChallengeRegistry state. No secret material is included. Used for monitoring and health checks.
postconditions: Returned snapshot reflects the registry state at the time of the call
No secret or sensitive material is included in the snapshot

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only snapshot

functions.name: get_endpoint_health
functions.description: Returns the current health status of the configured webhook endpoint. Used for monitoring, circuit breaker decisions, and operational alerting.

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.description: The endpoint to query health for

postconditions: Returned health reflects the current circuit breaker state and delivery statistics
No secret material is included in the health report

structured_side_effects.description: Read-only query

functions.name: rotate_webhook_secret
functions.description: Rotates the webhook signing secret for an endpoint. Moves the current_secret to previous_secret and installs a new current_secret. Both secrets remain valid for signature verification during the rotation window. The previous previous_secret (if any) is zeroized and dropped.

inputs.name: endpoint
inputs.description: The endpoint configuration to rotate secrets for

inputs.name: new_secret
inputs.description: The new signing secret to install as current_secret

error_cases.name: duplicate_key_id
error_cases.condition: new_secret.key_id matches the current secret's key_id

error_cases.name: weak_secret
error_cases.condition: new_secret.key_bytes is less than 32 bytes

postconditions: Returned config has new_secret as current_secret
Returned config has the old current_secret as previous_secret
The old previous_secret (if any) has been zeroized from memory
Both current_secret and previous_secret are valid for verify_webhook_signature

structured_side_effects.description: Rotates current_secret -> previous_secret, installs new current_secret, zeroizes old previous_secret
Records secret rotation event with old and new key_ids. No key material logged.

invariants: Pipeline timeout = deny (fail-secure): Any ChallengeHandle that reaches its deadline without a response MUST resolve as Denied with DenialReason::Timeout. There is no code path that resolves to Approved or Modified on timeout.

ANOMALY decisions are never silently resolved: Every AnomalyEscalation authorization request MUST produce an auditable AuthorizationDecision. The decision is never discarded, swallowed, or resolved without an audit record.

One-way data flow: Webhook payloads flow vault -> agent -> service (outbound notification). Callback responses flow service -> agent (inbound decision). No notification channel operation transmits vault plaintext or credential material.
No algorithm negotiation: HMAC-SHA256 is the only webhook signature algorithm. PASETO v4.local is the only token format. These are not configurable or negotiable per-endpoint.

Constant-time operations for all secret-dependent code paths: HMAC verification uses constant-time comparison. PASETO token verification uses constant-time operations (provided by rusty_paseto). No early-exit on signature mismatch.

Zero secrets in memory after use: All WebhookSecret instances implement ZeroizeOnDrop. PASETO symmetric keys are zeroized after token creation/verification. No secret material persists in memory beyond its operational scope.
Errors must not leak sensitive information: NotifyError variants contain generic messages. No raw key material, token content, or cryptographic intermediate values appear in error messages or logs.

Scope subset validation prevents privilege escalation: A Modify response's adjusted_scope MUST be a strict subset of the original requested_scope. Equal or superset scopes are rejected with ScopeEscalation error.
ChallengeHandle is consumed on resolution: Move semantics ensure resolve() can only be called once per handle. Double-resolution is a compile-time error, not a runtime check.
Webhook endpoints must use HTTPS: EndpointConfig.url is validated to start with 'https://'. HTTP endpoints are rejected at configuration time.

All disclosures are on the audit chain: Every notify(), resolve(), handle_callback(), and secret rotation produces an audit log entry with correlation IDs (event_id, challenge_id). No authorization decision occurs without an audit trail.
Newtype IDs are not interchangeable: ChallengeId, DeliveryId, and EventId are distinct types. Using the wrong ID type in an API call is a compile-time error.
requires: serde / serde_json (serialization with snake_case rename, deny_unknown_fields)
